text:
Once upon a time in a cozy little village called Evergreen, there lived a wise old owl named Ottmo. Ottmo loved all the creatures of the forest. He was particularly fond of three little owlets—you can imagine them—young things.

Every morning Ottmo would flutter outside and watch them play. They liked chasing things that squeaked. That's why they loved his beard feathers!

One day, an old wise cat named Mittens visited the forest. She'd hear all these tales of youthful mischief.

"What do you do all day?" Mittens inquired.

"Just watch children," Ottmo replied.

One particular afternoon, the owlets decided they needed an adventure. They fluttered off to find Mittens.

"You've got big eyes," they complimented her.

"They're very good eyes," she said pridefully.

That evening, Ottmo taught Mittens how to see something small. They watched a beetle. Mittens thought it was huge.

The next day, she practiced.
